notes: This is a high-convergence three-person working session (Sam, Vish, Paul) covering KPI strategy, first pass yield attribution, Microsoft debrief, security staging, and Franklin planning. The meeting converges strongly because the three participants operate with shared mental models, finish each other's reasoning, productively challenge each other's claims (Vish on FPY attribution, Sam acknowledging the senior tech confound), and close every thread with clear action items. The LSM is naturally high in a small group of trusted peers with deep shared context. Every topic opened is closed with alignment. | Caveats: This is a three-person inner-circle working session (Sam, Vish, Paul). Convergence is structurally easier with high-trust, high-context peers than in full-team meetings. The convergence score reflects the meeting as it is, but should not be compared directly to full-team sessions.; The meeting covers many topics (KPIs, FPY, Microsoft, security, Franklin, scheduling) with shallow depth on each. Convergence is broad but not deep — each topic gets aligned on direction but not fully resolved.; Sam's calendar/exhaustion disclosure is a vulnerability signal that functions as trust-building in this context. In a different group dynamic it could signal fragility.; Paul's extended Microsoft debrief includes colorful analogies (hot girl, Epstein) that are high-trust in-group humor. These would not work in a larger or more formal setting.
